channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Thu, 23 Jul 2026 09:23:30 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: “We’re told they…”: Candace Owens challenges Andrew Kolvet with ‘retraction’ demand in escalating Charlie Kirk feud
item.description: Candace Owens and TPUSA spokesman Andrew Kolvet publicly clashed after Kolvet questioned a Daily Mail report involving Erika Kirk. Owens responded by posting screenshots she said showed the newspaper requested comment before publication. Kolvet accused her of benefiting from the controversy, while Owens challenged TPUSA to seek legal action if the reported quotes were fabricated.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: US, Saudi Arabia sign civil nuclear deal paving way for uranium enrichment
item.description: The United States and Saudi Arabia signed a civil nuclear cooperation agreement. This pact allows for a joint study on uranium enrichment facilities. US officials stated the deal upholds safety and nonproliferation standards. Congress will review the agreement, facing potential opposition from lawmakers. The deal comes amid ongoing military operations against Iran.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: D4vd preliminary hearing: Celeste Rivas' family lawyer describes 'gruesome' crime scene photos
item.description: Singer D4vd's preliminary hearing revealed disturbing crime scene photos. Celeste Rivas' family lawyer described the evidence as gruesome and painful. Prosecutors allege D4vd lured Rivas to his home and dismembered her body. D4vd has pleaded not guilty to all charges against him. The case continues as the judge determines if it will proceed to trial.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Quote of the day by Laozi: 'A good traveler has no fixed plans and is not intent on arriving'
item.description: An old proverb suggests that a true traveler aims not for a destination, but for the adventure along the way. This philosophy highlights the value of immersing oneself in experiences, rather than merely seeking end goals. By concentrating on our journey, we become more adept at navigating surprises and changing landscapes. Modern psychology backs this notion, revealing that states of flow arise from genuine involvement in our pursuits.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Toughest thing I do': Donald Trump honours four US soldiers killed in Middle East
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote by Che Guevara: 'The life of a single human being is worth millions of times more than...'
item.description: Che Guevara emphasized that no earthly possession can match the value of a single human life. This profound statement confronts economic structures that disregard the sanctity of human life. In today’s world, challenges such as access to healthcare and adequate housing reveal entrenched tensions. Too often, profit motives overshadow the critical importance of safety and well-being, urging institutions to prioritize human survival above financial considerations.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: “Two families were destroyed": Erika Kirk and Candace Owens feud deepens as fresh claims emerge over Charlie Kirk case
item.description: Candace Owens has intensified her public feud with Erika Kirk, criticizing Charlie Kirk's widow over how she has handled questions surrounding his 2025 death. The remarks came as Erika attended court hearings for the accused gunman, with friends saying she remains focused on seeking justice rather than fueling public disputes.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: IShowSpeed and Carlos Alcaraz share fun dance moment at Justin Bieber’s 2026 World Cup afterparty
item.description: A video shared by @IShowSpeedHQ shows IShowSpeed and Carlos Alcaraz dancing together at Justin Bieber’s 2026 World Cup afterparty as a Michael Jackson song plays. Another photo captures Alcaraz in a Spain jersey holding the FIFA World Cup trophy. The clips give fans a fun look at two global stars celebrating Spain’s World Cup success together.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Scientists found a 2,865-pound predator from before dinosaurs ruled Earth
item.description: In an exciting collaboration, over 2,000 public volunteers are meticulously cleaning a colossal 1,300-kilogram fossilized skeleton of a Mastodonsaurus, an ancient amphibian that roamed the Earth 240 million years ago. Armed with specialized tools and ultraviolet light, these dedicated individuals are uncovering the mysteries of our planet's history. The spectacular skeleton is expected to be on display by October or November, showcasing humanity's connection to a distant past.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: A tapeworm infects ants and makes them live several times longer by turning their bodies into...
item.description: In Germany, tiny ants exhibit extended lifespans thanks to a parasitic tapeworm that cleverly alters their genetics to mimic queen ant biology. As these infected workers become less active and quieter in their nests, the parasite enhances its chances of survival by manipulating the ants' regulatory networks. This fascinating research sheds light on the complexities of genetic control related to aging processes.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Nabta Playa: The forgotten 7,000-year-old desert monument older than Stonehenge that may be the world’s first astronomical site
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: "Wild pursuit of fame and fortune": Candace Owens tears into Erika Kirk, claims Charlie Kirk was her "ticket to more fame"
item.description: Candace Owens intensified her public feud with Erika Kirk, accusing her of pursuing fame and fortune throughout her life. In a podcast, Owens claimed Charlie Kirk was Erika's "ticket to more fame" and argued a recent article criticizing her instead exposed Erika's character. She also questioned Erika's motives, saying her focus had always been on public attention, money, and influence.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: UAE launches instalment payment option for MoHRE service fees and fines via 'Tabby'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the Day by Greek philosopher Thales of Miletus: "Hope is the only good that is common to all men…"
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Pakistan seeks $10bn lifeline – 'brokerage fee' after mediation role between US & Iran
item.description: Pakistan seeks a ten billion dollar financial lifeline from the Trump administration. This facility aims to strengthen its foreign exchange reserves and ease rupee pressure. The request follows Pakistan's diplomatic role in facilitating US-Iran contacts. Economic stakes are considerable as Pakistan narrowly avoided default in 2023. The US is considering the proposal, which involves strategic and financial calculations.
item.dc:creator: Chidanand Rajghatta

item.title: Mexican illegal immigrant working on California farm arrested over rape and assault charges
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian trucker with legal California driver's licence arrested over $3 million stolen powder
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Ousted Venezuelan president Maduro to face US drug trafficking trial in June 2027
item.description: Ousted Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro and his wife will face trial in the United States. They are charged with drug trafficking and could receive life imprisonment if convicted. Their defense will challenge the indictment and the legality of their capture. US prosecutors allege Maduro facilitated cocaine shipments into the United States. The trial is scheduled to begin on June 1, 2027.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: The Sahara Desert was once filled with rivers, giant lakes and hippos. Scientists explain why it turns green every 20,000 years
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: How engineers dreamed of turning Egypt's Qattara Depression into an endless power source
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
